# Security & Regulatory-Integrity Checklist: Platform-Admin Portal — Invitations, Registration & Organisation Directory

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality (completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability)
for this feature's security-, safety-, and audit-integrity-relevant surface — the first
feature to expose a public, unauthenticated write endpoint that creates real tenant
organisations, and the first to add a second cross-tenant audit-attribution mechanism
alongside 013h's precedent.
**Created**: 2026-07-23
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-checklist` at the depth prior features in this pipeline applied
to safety/security-relevant work (013g, 013j, 025, 026) — these test the REQUIREMENTS, not the
implementation.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are attribution requirements defined for the invitation **create** action, not just resend/revoke? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008 vs Key Entities] — **Gap found**: FR-008 promises recording who performed create/resend/revoke, but the Key Entities section only lists revoke attribution fields; no creator attribution field is specified.
- [x] CHK002 Are rate-limiting / abuse-resistance requirements defined for the newly-public registration endpoint, given this feature is what first makes it reachable by real traffic? [Completeness, Gap] — **Gap found**: no such requirement exists anywhere in spec.md, despite the codebase's own precedent (feature 023's public enrollment form) treating an equivalent newly-public write endpoint as needing one.
- [x] CHK003 Are password-strength/validation requirements for the registration form referenced or restated, given this is the first time that existing rule becomes reachable through a real UI? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK004 Is a data-retention or minimum-visibility period defined for Invitation records containing a prospective director's PII (email) that are never accepted? [Completeness, Gap]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is "immediately usable" (SC-002) specific enough to be objectively verified (e.g. can log in with the just-submitted credentials with zero additional steps)? [Clarity, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK006 Is the exact set of invalid-token reasons that must produce one indistinguishable message enumerated completely (expired, revoked, already-used, never-existed)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK007 Is "read-only visibility only" (FR-013) precise enough to rule out even a future casual addition (e.g. an inline "copy email" button that silently also updates a "last viewed" timestamp)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK008 Is the derived-status model (FR-004) consistent with the Key Entities description of Invitation, given both must agree on exactly which underlying facts produce which status? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004 vs Key Entities]
- [x] CHK009 Does FR-006's "no distinction between explicit revoke and supersede-triggered revoke" stay consistent with FR-008's per-action attribution requirement — i.e. is it clear that a supersede is attributed to whoever performed the create/resend, not the invitation's original recipient or creator? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006 vs FR-008]
- [x] CHK010 Is the organisation directory's explicit read-only scope (FR-013) consistent with the Out of Scope section's tenant-suspension deferral, so a future reader doesn't mistake the directory's `provisioningStatus` display for an activation control? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012/013 vs Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK011 Can SC-004's "hard guarantee, not best-effort" for revoked/expired/reused-token rejection be objectively tested (e.g. via an explicit acceptance scenario covering reuse of an already-accepted token)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK012 Are the organisation directory's fields (FR-012) specific enough to generate an unambiguous contract (exact field list, not "relevant organisation details")? [Measurability, Spec §FR-012]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK013 Are concurrent-action scenarios (two platform-admins acting on the same invitation) addressed for every action (create/resend/revoke), not just resend-vs-revoke? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK014 Is the scenario of a platform-admin's own `IsPlatformAdmin` flag being revoked mid-session (e.g. by another platform-admin acting on the same account, if that were ever possible) addressed, or explicitly out of scope? [Coverage, Gap] — likely N/A (013h has no in-app way to revoke the flag at all), worth an explicit note rather than silence.
- [x] CHK015 Are recovery-flow requirements defined for the case where the invitation email fails to send after the Invitation row is already created (partial failure)? [Coverage, Exception Flow, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK016 Is the assumption that a 64-byte, cryptographically random token makes brute-force guessing infeasible (as partial justification for CHK002's rate-limiting question) explicitly documented anywhere, rather than left implicit? [Assumption, Gap]
- [x] CHK017 Is the dependency on feature 001's existing `RegisterOrganisationCommand` validator (org-name rules, password rules) explicitly named, so a future reader knows this feature does not duplicate or re-derive those rules? [Dependency, Spec Assumptions]

## Notes

- CHK001 (creation attribution) and CHK002 (rate limiting) were the two substantive findings —
  both fixed: spec.md gained FR-008's explicit creation-attribution clause and new FR-011a;
  data-model.md gained `CreatedByUserId`/`CreatedByEmail`; research.md gained R12/R13; tasks.md
  gained T008a-c and updated T001/T002/T009/T012; the API contract doc gained the
  `createdByEmail` field and the rate-limiting note on the registration endpoint. None deferred.
- CHK003 (password validation reference), CHK013 (broadened concurrency edge case), CHK015
  (email-send partial-failure), CHK016 (token-entropy assumption), CHK017 (validator dependency
  reference) were smaller clarity gaps, also fixed directly in spec.md (FR-009, Edge Cases, and
  three new Assumptions bullets).
- CHK004 (retention), CHK014 (mid-session flag revocation) were resolved by adding explicit
  Assumptions bullets stating they're deliberately out of scope, with reasoning — not silently
  left unaddressed.
- CHK005-CHK012 (clarity/consistency/measurability items) were re-checked against the spec as
  originally written and already passed — no change needed.
